verses.text: 'O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!' David marvels, for God ordains praise even from infants to silence His foes. The Creator's glory fills the world. He chooses the weak and small to confound the proud.

'O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!' David marvels, for God ordains praise even from infants to silence His foes. The Creator's glory fills the world. He chooses the weak and small to confound the proud.

Gazing at the moon and stars, David asks, 'what is man, that thou art mindful of him?' yet God has made him 'a little lower than the angels,' crowned with glory. Man is tiny, yet honoured. That the great God should mind such creatures is the wonder of grace.

Gazing at the moon and stars, David asks, 'what is man, that thou art mindful of him?' yet God has made him 'a little lower than the angels,' crowned with glory. Man is tiny, yet honoured. That the great God should mind such creatures is the wonder of grace.

Gazing at the moon and stars, David asks, 'what is man, that thou art mindful of him?' yet God has made him 'a little lower than the angels,' crowned with glory. Man is tiny, yet honoured. That the great God should mind such creatures is the wonder of grace.

God gave man dominion over all the works of His hands, and David closes as he began: 'how excellent is thy name!' Man's rule reflects God's gift. Fulfilled in Christ, the true Son of Man crowned with glory, this dignity is restored to all who are His.
